response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform; GIS work for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second using DLang with async I/O, fibers, and caching — skills that transfer well to routing pipeline work. However, modern C++ exposure is limited to a short 2014-2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, with no Rust experience listed, and recent roles (2020-present) center on TypeScript/Node.js product work rather than systems programming. There is no direct navigation/routing or map-tile pipeline experience, so the fit hinges on whether transferable systems and geospatial skills outweigh the gap in the primary language requirement.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G speaker)
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux/Kubernetes operations
concerns: Modern C++ experience is dated (2014-2015) and brief; no Rust experience listed
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline background
Recent roles (2020-present) are primarily TypeScript/web full-stack, away from systems-level work
